hero.eyebrow: Mineralogical Intelligence at the Loading Face
hero.headline: Chemistry defines where the ore goes. We read it at the face.
hero.subheadline: SpectraFlow AI classifies copper mineral species — oxide, sulfide, mixed — directly at the loading point using LIBS spectroscopy and Machine Learning. Chemical speciation, not color, defines whether material goes to leaching, flotation or the waste dump.
hero.trl: Maturity status: TRL 4 → laboratory validation in progress
hero.disclaimer: * Range to be validated with F2 reconciliation data from each deposit. ** Published LIBS+ML accuracy on minerals under laboratory conditions (bibliographic reference). Field accuracy is our validation target, not a guaranteed figure.

problem.headline: At the contact zone, Dispatch decides blind.
problem.intro: The short-term block model loses resolution exactly where it matters most: lithological contacts between oxide, sulfide and waste. The operator classifies visually, and every error sends high-grade ore to the dump — irreversibly.

cards.title: Resolution that falls short
cards.desc: Blocks of 5–10 m average out lithologies that change over centimeters. At the contact, that resolution is insufficient to decide the correct destination.

cards.title: F2 out of range
cards.desc: Mine-Plant reconciliation deviation exceeds operational excellence ranges, compromising short- and medium-term planning.

cards.title: Permanent loss
cards.desc: Misclassified fine copper that reaches the dump is not recovered. It is a direct, continuous loss, invisible in standard operational reports.

problem.honest_note: How much this problem costs depends on the deposit. We don't sell a generic figure: the first deliverable of our work is to quantify it with the real F2 reconciliation data from your operation.

approach.headline: We measure elemental chemistry. We don't infer color.
approach.intro: The technical difference that defines our edge: vision and hyperspectral technologies infer mineralogy from reflectance. LIBS measures the real elemental composition of the material — the same chemistry that determines its metallurgical route.

s1.title: Laser pulse on the material
s1.desc: A high-energy laser pulse vaporizes a micro-portion of the ore and generates a plasma. LIBS is a standard, mature technique for elemental analysis.

s2.title: ICCD spectrometer reads the signature
s2.desc: The gated detector captures emission lines of Cu, Fe, S and associated elements in microseconds. Each element has an unmistakable spectral signature.

s3.title: Proprietary model discriminates species
s3.desc: Our Machine Learning model, trained on the deposit's spectral library, classifies oxide / sulfide / mixed from the real elemental chemistry.

s4.title: Classification to the dispatch flow
s4.desc: The result is delivered to the management system to inform the material routing decision: leaching, flotation or waste.

approach.diff_title: Why LIBS and not the alternatives
approach.diff_intro: We don't compete by denying others. We position ourselves where LIBS is technically superior for the specific problem of copper speciation.

rows.limit: Infers mineralogy; does not provide quantitative elemental composition.
Does not detect light elements (Z<16); poorly discriminates Cu species.
Requires a spectral library per deposit (our barrier to entry).

science.headline: Technical honesty as competitive advantage.
science.intro: A serious evaluator trusts those who declare their real maturity status. This is what is proven, what we are validating, and what is our declared engineering challenge.

columns.title: LIBS + ML classifies minerals
items: Mineral classification by LIBS+ML reports 82–94% accuracy under laboratory conditions (peer-reviewed literature).
Elemental speciation of Cu, Fe and S by plasma spectroscopy is established physics.
Initiatives like CRC ORE + NRC Canada validate LIBS for mineral characterization in mining.

columns.title: Accuracy on the real deposit
items: Building the spectral library specific to the target deposit (matrix effect).
ML model v0 trained on real contact-zone samples.
Documented benchmark LIBS vs. hyperspectral vs. XRF on the same samples.

columns.title: Stand-off operation on the shovel
items: Measurement at 5–8 m on a moving boom is our underlying engineering challenge, not a solved premise.
The MVP validates first in a confined proximity configuration (safe and proven).
Stand-off is addressed as a separate optical problem, after validating the chemistry.

metrics.headline: Target metrics, not promises.
metrics.intro: These are the pilot's validation goals. We present them as objectives to be demonstrated with evidence, not as guaranteed results.

items.label: Target lab accuracy
items.desc: Oxide/sulfide classification on real deposit samples, validated against reference assays.

items.label: Target F2 deviation
items.desc: Stabilization of Mine-Plant reconciliation as the system's operational impact goal.

items.label: vs. existing technologies
items.desc: Documented technical comparison demonstrating LIBS superiority for copper speciation.

roadmap.headline: Validate cheap before investing big.
roadmap.subheadline: A disciplined stage-gate structure. Each phase has an evidence-based decision milestone. If a phase fails its criterion, the project stops or pivots before committing the next block of capital.

phases.title: Customer Discovery
phases.desc: 15+ interviews with Mine Superintendents and Geologists at target operations. Identification of internal sponsors and quantification of the problem's priority.
phases.gate: Gate: ≥3 sponsors interested in a POC

phases.title: Problem Quantification
phases.desc: Historical F2 reconciliation analysis + real face samples. Business case with data from the specific deposit, not generic estimates.

phases.title: Laboratory Validation
phases.desc: Proximity LIBS at a university lab on deposit samples + ML model v0 + benchmark against hyperspectral and XRF + academic-center endorsement.

phases.title: Letter of Intent + Safety
phases.desc: Preliminary system HAZOP, POC proposal with a CAPEX-to-client model, and letter-of-intent negotiation with the primary sponsor.
phases.gate: Gate: 1 signed LOI for a paid POC

totals.label: Validation MVP investment
totals.value: USD 68,000 · 5 months. Designed to produce defensible technical evidence, a client with a signed letter of intent and a competitive benchmark — before building the full system.

model.headline: Land & Expand: from diagnosis to system.
model.subheadline: We don't ask the operation for a blind capital bet. We enter with a low-risk service that delivers immediate value and opens the door to the full system.

cards.title: Geometallurgical Diagnosis
cards.desc: Quantification of misclassification loss with real F2 data. A low-cost, high-value consulting service that builds the relationship and the specific business case.

cards.title: POC with CAPEX-to-client
cards.desc: Proof of concept in a proximity configuration. The client acquires the hardware (their balance sheet supports it); Andean Flow provides the software, model and integration.

cards.title: License + Recurring Service
cards.desc: Classification software license + continuous model recalibration. The recurring value is in geometallurgical knowledge and data, not in reselling equipment.

team.headline: Geometallurgy, data and systems. The right combination.
team.intro: The project's real advantage is not the hardware — it's the team able to read the chemistry, model it and integrate it into the operation.

members.name: Miguel Giuliano
members.bio: Applied geometallurgy and short-term block modeling. Defines what to classify, designs the spectral library and validates that classification is geologically correct.

members.name: Cristian Jara
members.bio: Spectral classification algorithms in Python. Responsible for training, uncertainty quantification and model deployment on the Edge node.

members.name: Giovanni Giuliano
members.bio: Hardware-software integration in industrial mining environments. Responsible for the Edge node, the dispatch system interface and operational robustness.

team.advisors_note: In the process of onboarding: a mathematician specialized in chemometrics, a Wenco Fleet Control systems expert, and an industrial engineer with routing and ML experience.

vision.headline: SpectraFlow is the first step.
vision.subheadline: An ecosystem of solutions for the mining-metallurgical value chain, built on the same foundation: chemistry, data and geometallurgy.

units.title: SpectraFlow AI
units.desc: Mineralogical classification at the loading face by LIBS + ML. The product we are validating today.

units.title: Mine-Plant Reconciliation
units.desc: Geostatistics and volumetric reconciliation for estimation-error management in the block model.

units.title: Intelligent Traffic Control
units.desc: Real-time mining traffic management: congestion prediction and optimal routing. Second product under evaluation.

cta.headline: Let's talk about the first step.
cta.subheadline: We are looking for an operation willing to quantify the problem with its own data. That diagnosis is the low-risk start of a collaboration that can transform your site's reconciliation.

footer.tagline: Mineralogical intelligence. From the face, with scientific rigor.
footer.rights: © 2026 Andean Flow Technologies SpA. All rights reserved.
footer.disclaimer: The accuracy and return figures presented are validation targets based on scientific literature and reference cases, not guaranteed results. Empirical validation is the purpose of the piloting program.
